title: Oscars 2026 winners and recap: One Battle After Another named Best Picture
text: The 2026 Oscars ceremony delivered its expected outcome, with One Battle After Another winning Best Picture in a race that captivated industry observers and audiences alike. The ceremony began with Conan O'Brien's return as host, continuing his second consecutive year hosting the Academy Awards. O'Brien's monologue referenced Timothée Chalamet's controversial comments about opera and ballet while also addressing contemporary political themes subtly throughout the evening. Best Picture winner One Battle After Another was directed by Paul Thomas Anderson and featured Leonardo DiCaprio in a leading role. The film's supporting cast included Sean Penn, who won Best Supporting Actor for his performance. The Best Actor category went to Michael B. Jordan for his dual roles in the vampire thriller Sinners, marking his first Oscar win. This victory highlighted the strength of individual performances even when competing films don't win the top prizes. Sinners, despite its record 16 nominations, ultimately won four Oscars total. The difference between the films' performance reflected both the comprehensive nature of One Battle After Another's precursor support and the specific strengths of individual performances within Sinners. The ceremony also celebrated cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw, who made history as the first female cinematographer to win an Oscar.
